purpose: Orchestrate the agentic turn loop — receive user prompt, construct LLM request with system prompt segments, dispatch to provider, parse tool calls, execute tools, and loop until completion or compaction

activities.name: Receive User Prompt
activities.description: Accept user input via session.send RPC or interactive TUI input; validate and normalize into message structure

activities.name: Construct LLM Request
activities.description: Build messages array with system prompt segments (identity, tone, tool_efficiency, environment_context, code_change_rules, guidelines, safety, tool_instructions, custom_instructions, last_instructions), user message, and conversation history

activities.name: Call LLM Provider
activities.description: Dispatch request via model router (idef0.02); receive streaming response with assistant content and/or tool_use blocks

activities.name: Parse Tool Requests
activities.description: Extract tool calls from LLM response; classify as external (SDK-handled: read_file, edit_file, bash, etc.) or built-in (think, todowrite); validate parameters against tool schema

activities.name: Execute Tool Calls
activities.description: Dispatch each tool call to the tool system (idef0.04); apply permission policy (ask/auto-approve/deny); collect results with output content

activities.name: Check Loop Continuation
activities.description: If tool results exist, loop back to A2/A3 with results appended to context; if no tool calls in response, emit session.idle event and exit loop

activities.name: Handle Compaction
activities.description: If context token utilization exceeds threshold, trigger history.compact (summary-based or server-side) before next turn to stay within model context window

arrows.label: Tool definitions + system prompt segments (identity, tone, safety, etc.)
LLM response stream (assistant content + tool_use blocks)
Parsed tool call list (name, parameters, id)
Loop-back: tool results appended to conversation context
session.idle event (turn complete, no pending tool calls)
Context utilization check (token count vs threshold)
